# Code RED source/decompiled export
# Source: research/CodeRed_USArmy_Companion_AI_Research_Pass12.txt
# SHA1: 5e6b4bf9dfbb2837a4c3119b984889e667ac2312

Code RED — US Army Companion/Bodyguard AI Research Notes
Date: 2026-04-29
Scope: Check companion/follow AI, cutscene/mission references, faction relations, and safe routes for a 4-man US Army horseback bodyguard group.

SUMMARY
- A true always-on companion posse is possible only if we can bind actors to an external follow/squad/task objective or decode the compiled companion WSC layer.
- A resource-only approximation is possible now: spawn a low-density US Army/PlayerAlly posse pool near player-traveled regions, put horses beside them, make them friendly/ally to Player, and add TR pulses to follow/help/defend the player.
- The stock AI files prove the engine already supports TASK_FOLLOW_ACTOR, squad following, guard help, ally help, horse mounting, and combat defense.
- The stock content includes compiled companion and posse scripts, but they are RSC/WSC compiled resources, not clean text yet.
- The safest next patch should replace the Pass12 deputy companion proxies with a US Army companion pool and reinforce Player <-> USLawEnforcement / PlayerAlly relations.

PRIMARY AI FILES FOUND
1) content/ai/tasks.tr
   Strongest readable follow source.
   Important stock comments/tokens:
   - "TASK_FOLLOW_ACTOR script command"
   - ObjectClassObjectiveFollow(x)
   - CanFindAndFollow(x)
   - FindAndFollow(x)
   - FollowMove(x)
   - FollowLookAt(x)
   - FollowMatchSpeed(x)
   - FollowMatchSpeedHorseToFoot(x)
   - FollowMatchSpeedFootToHorse(x)
   - ObjectClassObjectiveMount(x)
   - PerformTaskMount
   - MoveToAndMount(x)
   - CanMountNearbyHorse

   Read: the game has real follow behavior for actors ordered by script/task/objective. TR can support the behavior once the objective exists, but TR alone may not create the follow objective.

2) content/ai/oldunused/squad.tr
   Strongest readable squad/formation source.
   Important stock tokens:
   - SquadLeader(x)
   - SquadMember(x)
   - SquadFollow(x)
   - SquadMemberToFollow(x)
   - FollowMatchSpeed(x)
   - FollowedBySquadMember(x)
   - FollowingSquadMember(y)
   - Defend leader rules exist in this file.

   Read: player-led squad behavior probably exists in native/task code, but this file is marked old/unused. Still useful as vocabulary/proof that squad following and leader defense are stock concepts.

3) content/ai/human_guard.tr
   Strongest readable ally-help source.
   Important stock tokens:
   - EnableHelpOthers
   - MayNeedHelp(x)
   - ShouldHelpIfNeeded(x)
   - CanHelp(x)
   - Help(x)
   - Ally(x)
   - Guard -> EnableHelpOthers

   Read: if our bodyguards are Ally/Friendly to Player and hear/see trouble, the guard/help system can reinforce defending behavior.

4) content/ai/game_main.tr
   Useful existing hooks:
   - !IsCompanion && Player(x) && !Ally(x) && !Friendly(x) ... VehicleExit ...
   - Guard -> EnableHelpOthers
   - !Riding && PrefersRiding && CanMountNearbyHorse -> MountNearbyHorse, SpeedJog, AutonomousFireIfAllowed

   Read: IsCompanion exists as a stock predicate. This means the engine has a distinction for companion actors, but setting that flag probably happens from compiled scripts/templates, not from TR alone.

5) content/ai/animal_main.tr
   Useful for horse behavior and player ownership/follow:
   - AnimalOwnedByActor(x)
   - AnimalMostRecentRider(x)
   - BaseShouldFollowOwner(x)
   - NotInTownShouldFollowOwner(x)
   - MoveTo(x), SpeedJog, SpeechEvent("AnimalMovingToPlayer")

   Read: rider-owned horse follow behavior is stock. For a mounted US Army posse, the best route is to spawn each soldier with/near horses and use PrefersRiding/CanMountNearbyHorse behavior.

COMPILED COMPANION / POSSE / CUTSCENE FILES FOUND
These exist but are compiled WSC/RSC-style files. They are valuable leads, but not yet safe direct patch targets until Code RED improves WSC decoding/rebuilding.

content.rpf paths:
- root/content/release64/scripting/gringo/gringobrains/gringobrainscripts/companion_brain.wsc
- root/content/release64/ambient/crimeresponse/event_law_repsonse_posse.wsc
- root/content/release64/0x72440E09/missions/fbi01/fbi01.wsc
- root/content/release64/0x72440E09/missions/fbi04/fbi04.wsc
- root/content/release64/0x72440E09/missions/fbi05/fbi05.wsc
- root/content/release64/0x62D87569/0xDCF649E3/mexarmy01/mexarmy01.wsc
- root/content/release64/0x62D87569/0xDCF649E3/mexarmy02/mexarmy02.wsc
- root/content/release64/0x62D87569/0xDCF649E3/mexarmy03/mexarmy03.wsc
- root/content/release64/0x62D87569/0xDCF649E3/mexarmy04/mexarmy04.wsc
- root/content/release64/0x62D87569/0xDCF649E3/mexarmy05/mexarmy05.wsc
- root/content/release64/scripting/gringo/commonscripts/mexarmy_camp02_gringo.wsc
- root/content/release64/scripting/gringo/commonscripts/mexarmy_camp04_gringo.wsc
- root/content/release64/scripting/gringo/commonscripts/mexarmy_camp05_gringo.wsc

strings_d11generic.rpf relevant string keys:
- Companion_Marshal
- Companion_OutLaw
- Companion_Rebel
- Companion_FBI
- BLIP_POSSE_LEADER
- BLIP_DUTCH
- BLIP_JAVIER
- BLIP_BILL
- FBI01_FollowMe2Roof_*
- FBI01_MeetWithPosse_*
- FBI01_RideOutDutch_*
- FBI05_companionDead
- FBI05_NoFollowToCar_*
- AmericanSoldier_abandoned
- AmericanSoldier_attacked
- AmericanSoldier_dead
- AmericanSoldier_horse_attacked
- AmericanSoldier_horse_dead
- AmericanSoldier_horse_killed
- AmericanSoldier_horse_toomany
- AmericanSoldier_killed
- AmericanSoldier_return
- AmericanSoldier_toomany
- tutorial_law_army_spawn

Read: the game definitely has story/cutscene/mission concepts for companion following, posse meetings, Dutch/Bill/Javier blips, companion death, AmericanSoldier state lines, and army spawning tutorials. The direct control is likely in compiled WSC mission scripts.

act.rpf companion/action resources found earlier:
- act/companion/marshal.wat
- act/companion/outlaw.wat
- act/companion/mexicanhenchman.wat
- act/companion/mexicanrebel.wat
- act/companion/graverobber.wat
- custom/fbi01_gang.wat
- custom/fbi05_gaurd_companion.wat
- horse.wat
- wagon.wat

Read: act.rpf has companion animation/action resources, but WAT is still binary/RSC style. Use as reference for names/actions, not patch target yet.

US ARMY / SOLDIER IDENTITY LEADS
The tune/string data exposes these actor/speech identity names:
- AE_Caucasian_Army_Easy01
- AE_Caucasian_Army_Easy02
- AE_Caucasian_Army_Easy03
- AE_Caucasian_Army_Medium01
- AE_Caucasian_Army_Medium02
- AE_Caucasian_Army_Medium03
- AE_Caucasian_Army_Hard01
- AE_Caucasian_Army_Hard02
- AE_Caucasian_Army_Hard03
- AE_Mexican_Army_Easy01/02/03, Medium01/02/03, Hard01/02/03

Potential population pool for next pass:
PedWithEquipmentRandom "ped_codered_usarmy_bodyguard_posse"
  -> ped_codered_usarmy_bodyguard_01
  -> ped_codered_usarmy_bodyguard_02
  -> ped_codered_usarmy_bodyguard_03
  -> ped_codered_usarmy_bodyguard_04
  -> ped_generic_horse / ped_codered_army_bodyguard_horse beside them

Potential specific entries:
PedWithEquipmentSpecific "ped_codered_usarmy_bodyguard_01"
{
    ActorTemplate "template_law_WesternDeputy" or another verified law/combat template
    ActorEntities 1 { AE_Caucasian_Army_Easy01 }
}

Caution: AE_Caucasian_Army_* names are confirmed strings/actor identities, but not yet proven as valid ActorEntities inside level.pop. If they fail, fall back to template_law_WesternDeputy + Deputy entities first, then keep searching for the true AmericanSoldier template/entity source.

FACTION RELATION NOTES
Original factionrelations.xml has:
- PlayerAlly as a clean faction that is Ally to Player and PlayerMpTeamCoop.
- USLawEnforcement as law enforcement, enemy to gangs/criminals, ally to itself, friendly to USBystander.
- Player does not explicitly list USLawEnforcement in the original relation block.
- USLawEnforcement does not explicitly list Player in the original relation block.

Next pass should add/ensure:
- Player -> USLawEnforcement = Friendly or Ally
- USLawEnforcement -> Player = Friendly or Ally
- PlayerAlly -> USLawEnforcement = Ally/Friendly
- USLawEnforcement -> PlayerAlly = Ally/Friendly
- PlayerAlly/USLawEnforcement -> gang factions = Enemy
- gang factions -> PlayerAlly/USLawEnforcement = Enemy

This is necessary so US Army bodyguards do not treat the player as a crime target and will defend against gang enemies.

WHAT IS POSSIBLE NOW WITHOUT WSC COMPILING
A practical Pass13/Pass14 resource-only implementation can do:
1) Replace Pass12 deputy companion proxies with a US Army bodyguard pool.
2) Spawn 4 companion candidates plus 4 horses in major regions and wilderness/town-border areas.
3) Use a low but persistent density, enough to respawn if some die.
4) Add TR pulse:
   - Player(x) && Ally(x) && !SpatialDistanceClose(x) && CanFindAndMoveTowards(x) -> FindAndMoveTowards(x), SpeedJog
   - Player(x) && Ally(x) && SpatialDistanceClose(x) && CanHelp(x) -> Help(x)
   - !Riding && PrefersRiding && CanMountNearbyHorse -> MountNearbyHorse, SpeedJog, AutonomousFireIfAllowed
   - Enemy(x) && CanCombatShootNoCover(x) -> CombatShootNoCover(x)
5) Keep factionrelations aligned so the posse defends the player and attacks gangs.

WHAT IS NOT GUARANTEED YET
- "At all times" exact persistence.
- Exact spawn beside the player.
- Exact formation around player.
- Exact respawn immediately after death.
- Exact US Army visual models if AE_Caucasian_Army_* are not accepted as ActorEntities.

Those probably require either:
- compiled WSC companion_brain/task follow patching, or
- finding a parent population/entity file that binds Player as an ObjectiveFollow target, or
- discovering a direct companion/spawn manager in the mission scripts.

BEST NEXT PASS PLAN
Pass13 — US Army Companion Posse Test
- Use Pass12 as baseline.
- Add a new drop-in folder: DROP_IN_US_ARMY_BODYGUARD_POSSE.
- Patch factionrelations.xml for Player/USLaw/PlayerAlly friendliness.
- Add ped_codered_usarmy_bodyguard_posse with 4 soldiers and 4 horses.
- Replace the earlier Dutch/Javier/Bill proxy names with honest US Army names.
- Add CodeRedUSArmyCompanionPulse into tune/content game_main.tr.
- Add validation checking the pool exists, 4 soldier refs exist, horse refs exist, and faction relations are present.

Higher-risk later pass:
- Decode/rebuild companion_brain.wsc or event_law_repsonse_posse.wsc to set true follow/squad objective against Player.
